properties.name: Central Doha
properties.description: The historic downtown combining the Corniche waterfront promenade and the Msheireb regeneration district. The bay-facing edge benefits from sea breezes and irrigated greenery; the denser inland blocks retain more heat due to compact street layouts and limited canopy.

properties.name: West Bay Lagoon
properties.description: A residential and mixed-use district centred on a circular artificial lagoon west of the CBD. Open water and some landscaping moderate surface temperatures noticeably below the fully paved districts immediately to the east.

properties.name: West Bay
properties.description: Qatar's central business district, dominated by glass-and-steel towers, wide asphalt roads, and minimal natural ground cover. The high density of impervious surfaces makes this one of the city's most persistent heat zones.

properties.name: The Pearl-Qatar
properties.description: A luxury artificial island with Gulf exposure on all sides. Coastal breezes and curated landscaping keep surface temperatures among the lowest of Doha's dense urban districts.

properties.name: Industrial Area
properties.description: Qatar's primary logistics and manufacturing belt, covered almost entirely by warehouse rooftops and hardstanding. The city's lowest greenery index and highest recorded surface temperatures — driven by dark surfaces and zero shade.

properties.name: Al Thumama
properties.description: A transitional district between central Doha and Hamad International Airport, mixing residential blocks with logistics infrastructure. Greenery is limited to isolated gardens, keeping temperatures above the city median.

properties.name: Aspire Zone
properties.description: A 250-hectare sports and recreational park anchored by Khalifa International Stadium. Maintained athletic fields and tree cover create a local cool island — one of the greenest zones within the inner urban boundary.

properties.name: Al Waab
properties.description: An established suburban residential area between Education City and central Doha. Irrigated villa gardens and some street trees sustain moderate greenery, though daytime surface temperatures remain elevated compared to park zones.

properties.name: Education City
properties.description: Qatar Foundation's 2,500-hectare academic campus with irrigated lawns, tree-lined boulevards, and low building density. The most consistently vegetated large district in western Doha, reliably cooler than surrounding areas.

properties.name: Lusail City
properties.description: A fast-growing planned city to Doha's north. Young street trees and an abundance of new concrete and asphalt push surface temperatures toward the higher end of the suburban range despite its modern design.

properties.name: Al Wakrah
properties.description: A historic fishing town turned residential suburb on Doha's southern coast. Lower building density and coastal proximity moderate surface temperatures, and established neighbourhoods carry more mature greenery than newer northern developments.
